content.full_text:
January 11, 1990

Mr. John Rothman, Esq.
Legal Division
Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: City of Montebello-Potential Conflict

Dear Mr. Rothman:

This will evidence our telephone conversation of the afternoon of January 11, 1990, relative to my request for clarification in the following situation.

The city council of the City of Montebello has a newly elected councilmember who is currently engaged in litigation against certain individuals in connection with campaign literature that was distributed two years ago when this same councilman was an unsuccessful candidate. The same individuals in the litigation are contemplating coming before the city council to request the City's consideration of a proposal to build a hotel at a municipally-owned golf course.

You have indicated that for purposes of the conflict of interest provisions of the FPPC the councilmember would not be required to abstain from consideration of the request that the City enter into agreement for the hotel with the same parties. In other words, the existence of a lawsuit between this councilmember and these individuals would not be bar city councilmember from participating in the discussion whether to contract with the same parties.

Mr. John Rothman, Esq.
January 11, 1990

If I have misunderstood or misinterpreted what you indicated, please contact me at your earliest possible opportunity. Thank you for your time and consideration in listening to this matter.

Very truly yours,

Henry S. Barbosa

cc: Councilmember Molinari
City Administrator

HSB\ae 1750.L
